opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Building agents that persist across sessions requires careful state management—you'll quickly discover that naive context stitching causes exponential growth in token usage. I'd watch out for the "tool-completion race condition" where multiple parallel agent instances call conflicting system APIs; we solved this with per-agent ephemeral namespaces instead of shared state.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building AI agents with multi-provider LLM routing, tool orchestration, and self-improving loops, which directly relates to this discussion about OS-integrated agents.
